name: Peruvian Chicken with Green Sauce (Aji Verde)
why: Peruvian pollo a la brasa is a vibrant new cuisine territory — soy-vinegar-marinated chicken roasted to golden crispness, served with a fiery cilantro-jalapeño green sauce that's addictive on everything. The aji amarillo and aji panca pastes deliver authentic Peruvian flavor you can't replicate with standard spices.
author: Henry
ingredients: 1 small whole chicken (3.5-4 lbs), backbone removed and halved
1 packed cup cilantro leaves and thinner stems
Salt and pepper to taste (for green sauce)

steps: In a bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons olive oil, soy sauce, distilled white vinegar, kosher salt, black pepper, aji amarillo paste, aji panca paste, and grated garlic until smooth.
Remove the backbone from the chicken (or ask your butcher to spatchcock it) and split the chicken in half.
Pat the chicken dry with paper towels, then place it skin-side up in a flat baking dish.
Spread the marinade generously over the outside of the chicken AND under the skin on all sides — getting marinade under the skin is key for maximum flavor.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 6 hours, but preferably overnight for the best results.
Place cilantro, chopped jalapeños, mayonnaise, lime juice, distilled vinegar, 2 garlic cloves, dried oregano, and 2 tablespoons olive oil into a food processor.
Blend until smooth and well combined.
Taste and season with salt and pepper as needed. For a hotter sauce, add another jalapeño and blend again.
About 1 hour before roasting, remove the chicken from the fridge to take the chill off.
Preheat the oven to 475°F (245°C) and set the rack to the middle level.
Pat the chicken dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture — this helps the skin crisp up.
Place the chicken halves skin-side up on a wire rack set over a baking sheet or roasting pan.
Rub the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil all over the skin.
Roast at 475°F for 10 minutes to start browning the skin.
Reduce heat to 425°F (215°C) and continue roasting until the internal temperature at the thickest part of the breast reaches 160°F (71°C) — approximately 35 more minutes, 45 minutes total.
Use a digital thermometer for accuracy — don't guess.
Transfer the chicken to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes before carving — this keeps the juices inside.
Carve the chicken into serving pieces and arrange on a platter.
Serve with the green sauce on the side for dipping.
Pair with Peruvian fries, rice and beans, or crusty bread for soaking up the sauce.

pro_tips: Marinate overnight — the soy sauce, vinegar, and Peruvian pastes need time to penetrate deep under the skin. This is what makes the chicken truly exceptional versus a quick roast.
The aji verde sauce is addictive on everything — don't just use it on chicken. Slather it on rice and beans, tostones, fried plantains, or even grilled vegetables.

Aji amarillo and aji panca pastes can be ordered online (Amazon, Peruvian specialty shops). If you can't find them, substitute with a mix of yellow chili paste and smoked paprika, but the authentic pastes make a real difference.

name: Creamy Garlic Shrimp Pasta
why: 20-minute weeknight winner — tender shrimp and al dente pasta in a creamy garlic sauce with roasted red peppers and zucchini. Quick, satisfying, and completely doable after a long day.
author: Henry
ingredients: 1/4 tsp each dried basil, marjoram, rosemary, thyme

steps: Boil a large pot of salted water. Cook pasta until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Melt butter in a large sauté pan over medium-high heat.
Add onion; cook 4-5 mins until soft.
Add garlic; cook 30 seconds.
Sprinkle flour over onions. Season with salt & pepper. Cook 1 minute, stirring frequently.
Toss in zucchini and roasted red peppers. Cook a few minutes until slightly softened.
Add heavy cream, lemon juice, herbs, and shrimp. Cook ~5 minutes, stirring frequently, until shrimp turns pink.
Stir in Parmesan cheese.
Remove pan from heat. Add cooked pasta and toss thoroughly to coat.
Serve immediately with extra Parmesan.

pro_tips: Add ~1/4 cup reserved pasta water to the cream mixture for better sauce texture.
Always grate Parmesan from a block — pre-grated doesn't melt the same way.
Keep frozen peeled shrimp on hand for ultra-quick weeknight meals.
